reviews.review_description: Great interactive game,but i need adding German audio language...pls devs

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the positive review! We’re glad you enjoyed the game.
We’re definitely hoping to add more audio languages in the future, including German. Thanks for the suggestion and for your support!

reviews.review_description: This is an amazing experience. It's more of an interactive movie than a game but its done extremely well. I'd definitely buy more experiences like this.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the review. It means a lot to us.

reviews.review_title: Sci-fi Live action with stake on AI
reviews.review_description: There are not many games like these but the story was interesting so it kept me going to find out what lies further ahead.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review!

reviews.review_description:
Great interactive story. Great cinema on a higher lvl.

One of this games, that listed on your AllTimeFavorite list.

Cinematic Adventure from Germany. Best interactive game. 

Game of the day or better game of the year 2025.

This is one of my all-time favorites. This is the reason why I sent so many reports. 

VR needs this. This is Meta Platforms Technologies as it`s best.

developer_response.body: Happy to hear you had a good time! We're humbled by your review and it means a ton to our team. :)

reviews.review_description:
Hopefully people will gravitate more to this
 because these dev need to make more of this they get. An interactive move where they manage to keep u stimulated in static situation. Love what you guys are and hope this is just the beginning.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, we're glad you enjoyed your time playing through Human Within! We're always looking for new opportunities in the VR space, but we're also trying our hand at several other genres. :)

reviews.review_description:
I'm a developer, though not VR but looking.  Having said that, I have been conceptually banging my head off how to immerse a player into a realistic story where the player does not feel like he is being given "busy work".
I'm 71 years old and remember a silly TV cartoon called Winky Dink where the child had to put a cellophane sheet over the TV screen and draw over the dotted lines to help Wink Dink escape a villain.  Winky Dink came out in 1953 .  Even with the incredible technological advancements, immersion VR is still Winky Dink!

Having said that, this is still an incredible experience.  It is probably close to the best we can expect at present.  With dynamic integration of AI characters, that is all going to change, soon!

developer_response.body: Happy to hear you enjoyed the experience and we totally share your enthusiasm for the direction VR is headed towards in the near future!

reviews.review_description: Human Within brilliantly utilises Virtual Reality to create an engaging interactive movie experience. It weaves a captivating narrative about the effects of AI and advanced technology on society, highlighting the potential dangers when such power is misused. The experience combines traditional movie elements with 360-degree visuals, allowing players to make choices that shape the story’s direction, along with some gaming features that enhance your connection to the narrative and its world. With five different endings based on your decisions, there’s plenty of incentive to replay the game. Players are likely to return, especially with the stunning visuals, immersive audio, and outstanding performances that elevate the experience.

developer_response.body: Thank you TheVRRealm for your early interest in this project and being one of the first to take it for a spin and share your experience with the community. We hope that if you enjoyed your first playthrough, you will play it again making different choices and exploring different outcomes.

reviews.review_description: Behind some cheesy dialogue and an unintuitive mechanic for making choices, Human Within is an enjoyable experience with likable characters and interesting game mechanics. While it doesn’t have much to say about it’s AI driven concept, it offers exciting scenarios and enough diverging paths to make at least one replay worthwhile.

developer_response.body: LoganS99 as one of our first players and reviews, thank you for your interest in Human Within. Indeed there are more outcomes to this story to explore and we'd encourage you to experience them all and share which path was your favourite.

reviews.review_description: This hinges on the story b/c your interactivity is limited & it (mostly) delivers. The whole premise of being a consciousness in cyberspace was really interesting (reminded me of Republique) and the interactive puzzles were fun. Plus multiple choices & endings adds replay value. I will absolutely be playing again. Livestreamed my first playthrough and viewers agreed it's worth a go. This is NOT for VR action junkies. It's an interactive story through and through, but I really dug it.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and support, LiveOpenMike! So glad you enjoyed your playthrough and plan to explore more of Human Within's original branching story.

reviews.review_title: No Choices or Choose/Hidden Door Glitch
reviews.review_description: I've come to the "choice" first in the game, now in the menu option portion in the game, and there's nothing on the controllers that allows any option but whatever the game does. I've attempted it twice, and there's no way to choose anything other than what the game decides. During the timer for which to make a choice, (I first) missed the timer, so the game played out; second time... I hit every available button on the controllers before the timer expired, and I got the same game result. I played to the second menu item for making a choice or choosing a new path in the game... and no buttons actives a change in the game. Nothing is worth finding the thief three times, and going through the frustration of repeating the same gameplay three times... it is torture... It's not a game, and it's definitely not any fun! Second, I inspected every inch of the room with the "hidden door," and it never revealed itself... never! I inspected every single inch; under cabinets, behind every curtain and up and down every single wall! I spend way too much time there. It was definitely a glitch there. All these accounted... this game totally sucks to the highest hell. This was some joke of a game, huh? I'd absolutely hate opening it up again unless the developers have a specific, detailed explanation of what you did. How do options or choosing become part of the game, and what button(s) operate that function before the timer expires? I totally hate your game. I really hope I explained myself and we are clear! Thank you for your support! I personally believe that you experimented on me with this game, and you charged me for your failure!

developer_response.body:
Hey LeviAndrewLane, we're sorry to hear you did not enjoy your initial playthrough of Human Within, but we would very much like to help address the challenges you experienced with the Choice Moments and Point Cloud exploration. We just released an update that provides a tutorial on how the choice mechanic works. Here is an explanation to help you out... 

When the choice UI appears, there is no button to press on the controller. Instead, the choice is made by holding your gaze in the direction of the character the choice is based on. Under the timer meter, <<<< and >>>> arrows indicate the direction of the other choice. By turning your head in the indicated direction you can see what the other option is. 

Making a choice is done by looking at a character until the timer completely fills in. While the timer is short, you can look back and forth between the options to reset the timer each time. 

For the Point Cloud room with the hidden door, it would seem that you may have encountered a bug. When searching the room, were you looking at things and surfaces or were you pointing at them with the controller, too? The hidden door should become highlighted when pointing the controller at it. We will investigate it on our end to see if there is a bug with it. We hope this helps. 

You can reach out to us at emails@signalspace.ca anytime, if you would like to know more.

reviews.review_description:
It's cool to see the merging of VR and film and what's here is relatively unique though technical limitations and mixed use of VR plus the overall "indie" feel of this project does leave much to be desired here.  Still a cool experience, but a flawed one.

Full Review - https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCYEe3aKUbYO43egsBUX8dKQ

developer_response.body: Thank you The_VR_Grid for your early interest and review of Human Within

reviews.review_description:
Human Within is an interactive narrative game -- the emphasis is on the narrative, but there's lot of interaction, including user choices which determine how the narrative proceeds.

I've only played partway through the game, but the story is gripping and I really care what happens to the two main characters.
The one flaw I originally found in the game is that when I was offered a choice, I couldn't figure out how to indicate what choice  wanted to make.  I was going to give the game an unfavorable review, but I decided to contact the developers first.  Here's how they explained things to me.

When you have a choice to make, you see a brief text describing the default choice and some moving arrows.  If you look in the direction that arrows point, you'll see what the alternate choice is.  If you want the alternate choice, keep looking at the alternate, and the game will confirm your choice (no buttons to press).  If you want the default, just look in the other direction.

There's a brief timer to make your choice, but every time you look in the direction of one of the choices, the timer resets.

If you mess up, no problem. Just hit the game menu button on the left controller and choose to restart the current scene.

The developers should have explained all this in the game, but they tell me that they're going to add a brief tutorial in an update explaining how to make choices.

developer_response.body: Hi David, happy to hear we were able to help on this front. Additionally, the update with said tutorial has been pushed to the live version. Let us know if it's clear enough or if you have any other feedback! :)

reviews.review_description: How can our engaging can improve looking contents like this? I personally think that this can be the future of all the fundamentals challenges that we have in order to engage people and improve real CTA.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, glad you enjoyed your time playing Human Within!

reviews.review_title: Fantastic concept, Needs a little polish
reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed this experience and loved the concept of being inside someone’s head and making choices to help her and her sister escape.

The interactions with the cubes weren’t intuitive enough. I wanted to move the cubes and leave them in the space I left them in, to check what I had. Unfortunately they pinged back into original position so found it frustrating. 


I also didn’t have any idea how to make different choices and pressed every button but nothing happened. Perhaps an on screen prompt could help here. 

There were some cringy moments, especially with the restaurant scene. No one seemed to mind a grown man throwing a plate across the floor. It was weird. 

The 360 camera was an interesting idea but the quality just isn’t there yet.  

After all said and done, it was a very innovative game idea. I can see this style of game being fantastic for VR in the future. I congratulate the devs for trying something new.

developer_response.body: JEN.VRSTAR Thank you for sharing your experience and review of Human Within. You are one of the first to do so! The feedback on puzzle solving has been noted. Regarding those choice moments, this experience uses "Gaze Control" prompted by what/where you focus via the headset to make those decisions. Hope that helps with your next playthrough!

reviews.review_description:
Full review available by searching Gamertag VR on YouTube 

Human Within is kinda of new thing for VR. Mixing 2D high resolution video, 360 stereoscopic video and Virtual reality all into one experience. I’m sure by now you’ve worked out from the trailer this is more of an interactive movie and I went in not expecting to review it but it did peak my interest enough to stay in til the end. The story never really excites you. You don’t feel worried for the characters and this makes the whole experience a bit forgettable, but on the other hand I’m kinda glad I got to experience it. Something like this would be good as more of a rental service application for interactive movies for say $5 rather than pay the full asking price and as of right now I would really only recommend it to people desperate to experience something unique, but it’s also a genre I would like to see expand. VR is great way to bring the user into a world but when your left as more of a bystander, there needs to be some serious emotional connection and high interest involved to keep you in the film and Human Within lacks both of these. It’s clever, well thought out and aside from the 360 video being a bit low resolution, is well presented, but overall I give human within a wait for a sale recommendation.

developer_response.body: Thank you Gamertag_VR for sharing your review and experience with us and the community. Much appreciated.

reviews.review_title: A Step In The Right Direction.
reviews.review_description: It's gratifying to see someone attempt to push the boundaries of FMV  experiences . It's a brave attempt, not entirely "there" yet, but definitely  a step in the right direction.  "Human Within" is an interactive movie with a mixture of 360 degree video, 2d videos held in V.R settings and pure V.R. The interactive elements  mainly consist of choices made during the 360 video section (made by pointing your gaze to the chosen option), 3D block puzzles and V.R recreations of spaces in the game where you can wind time backwards or forwards. I won't tell you anything about the narrative reasons for any of these elements, because I can't without spoliers. I will say that whilst they aren't always great( the block puzzles got old for me pretty fast), they definitely help immerse you in the story and make you feel as if you have some "skin in the game" so to speak. The story itself isn't wildly original, is quite simple, but I did enjoy it and I did find the protagonists likeable and engaging, the acting was above average, I felt invested until the end and even got a little tingle from an ending that was by no means groundbreaking or unexpected, but still gratifying. Of course, that was just MY ending(or my 1st ending) as the story of "Human Within" changes based upon the choices that you make. Those who have played flat screen FMV games will be familiar with the way this works, as will those who have seen the "Bandersnatch"  Black Mirror episode. There are several possible endings, Everything is presented nicely and the main V.R setting of the game looks fantastic and put me in mind of the movie "The Lawnmower Man".  The room recreations look awesome too. The weakest part visually is the 360 video, but I was expecting that. Still, It's a lot better quality than most I've seen. The game has a nice embedded theme tune (It's the "motivation" song for a character in the game) and when it came up at the end I realized I actually quite liked it.l>wordlimitreached<

developer_response.body: bramblevertigen, thanks for playing through the experience and sharing your review! Your feedback is appreciated and helpful. Sounds like you're going to explore the other outcomes based on some different choices which is great.

reviews.review_description:
I wanted to love it. At first it was intriguing, to me. The cubes stay the same (just getting harder). Maybe make different puzzles to keep the gamer interested? 

I don't want to be negative. Graphics amazing but it felt like a DVD, where the words didn't align eith the characters oral movements. 

It was somewhat cheesy. I need more stimulation, besides blocks. Great concept though!

developer_response.body: Thank you Medocire for taking an early interest in Human Within and sharing your impressions of the experience, your feedback on puzzle solving in particular.

reviews.review_title: A great example of what can be done.
reviews.review_description: Overall, I really liked it. There were parts early on where the acting was a little cheesy, but it gets better. The overall presentation was striking. The interface really immerses you in the film. The puzzles get a little redundant eventually, because it's basically the same type of puzzle, that just gets more difficult, but still fun. I guess the room scanning is a second type of puzzle, and that's pretty good. Being able to watch different views and scenes at once really gives you the feel that you're big brother watching everything. I just finished my first playthrough, but now I'm going to try some different choices and see how it affects the ending. Looking forward to it

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review T-Prime! We appreciate the support and time you've invested. We're very happy to hear that you enjoyed the style variety and are planning to explore the multiple different outcomes of the story.

reviews.review_description: This game is essentially an interactive movie. 90% of it is watching 3D VR video, and 10% is completing short puzzles and interactive tasks. As long as you know going into this going into the game and are okay with it, it's a great, polished experience. As of 3/25/25, 1400 players started playing the game and 406 finished it (according to the in-game achievement system). Give it a try! 👍🏻🎉

Ill try not to moan too much but ill just be honest.

The look and style of the game is awesome, i instantly felt like i was in some kind of Matrix / Tron themed interactive movie.

Apolgies if the actors are game devs, but it came across mostly as very cringy like the straight to dvd movies that cost minimal to make, really doesnt pull you into each scene.

The part where i had to stop playing was likw the 6th time in probably less then an hour that i had to put some cubes together with dots on.

I dread to think how many more times this mini game comes up but there really needed to be a variety and not just 1, it gets boring very fast when all it is is to move onto the next video to watch. 

Lastly for people who do purchase this, some kind of tutorial around how you decide the 2 options in the videos would be helpful.

The first scene in the room it just popped up some text out of nowhere, i noticed a bar going down with seconds 2 decide, i look up and down at the 2 lines of text and next minute apparently i had chosen an option!

I then restarted the scene to figure out what i was supposed to do, (had to watch the whole scene play out again unable to skip to the option / branch) then i realised i was supposed to look left or right.

It would make more sense to not have the timer, especially on the first ones, and you look left or right and press trigger to select.

Also the audio in the scene where they are doing the presentation was very odd, the voices were coming out the left speaker even though they was stood central / to the right, to the point that i kept looking out the door at first thinkin the person talking was 1 of the people in the corridor.

I cant see myself loading it back up so i will most likely refund it shortly.

A shame as the idea / concept is a good idea

developer_response.body:
Beatz, 
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed and honest feedback. We really appreciate you pointing out both what worked for you and what didn’t.

Since the early version you played, we’ve made several improvements directly based on player feedback. We added clear tutorials at the beginning to better explain how choices work in the 360° scenes, and we also added a skip option for the cube minigame in the menu. For the other minigames, once the solution is known, they can now be fast-forwarded to reduce repetition.

Our goal with these changes was to make the experience smoother and more respectful of the player’s time, and we genuinely hope they help improve the overall experience.

We completely understand if the game still wasn’t the right fit for you, but we truly appreciate you giving it a try and taking the time to help us improve through your feedback.

reviews.review_description: The idea to combine VR and movie elements is an interesting concept and definetly has potential. I am excited to what is coming in the future and if we will get to play through stories similar to that of Nyla and Linh.

developer_response.body: Thank you trotzkindvr we are also excited about the potential for immersive storytelling in VR and are happy you enjoyed your time with Human Within!

reviews.review_title: Good on devs for trying something new
reviews.review_description: I'm only about 45 minutes in but so far I'm enjoying it, the story, the concept.  The controls for me took a bit of getting used to but now its fine. I hope it's successful and they continue on with this sort of game play, a sequel or a different plot, but add the types of puzzle's with the cubes and ID'ing people.

developer_response.body: Paulsrift, thank you for your enthusiasm and support for Human Within. We appreciate the early review and sharing your experience with the community. Each playthrough of Human Within is different based on your choices so we hope you continue to play and enjoy it further.

reviews.review_title: A New Brilliant Way to Interact with VR
reviews.review_description:
Human Within is my new favorite way to interact with VR games. You direct the way the "movie" plays out by making choices throughout the story. I think this opens the door for more games like this. I would probably watch more movies this way if I could.

One minor thing I wasn't happy about. The puzzles but that's just me lol. I spent way too long on one of them and had to stop for a bit, then get back in there. 

So far, I've played three different endings, and I really can't wait to discover the other ways this plays out. I would love to see more like this!

It really doesn't hurt to give this a try.

developer_response.body: TheGamingVeteran, we appreciate the review and your enthusiasm for interactive narrative VR. We are thrilled you chose to play Human Within and it's great to hear you have already explored three of the the five endings. Two more to go!

reviews.review_title: Is Human Within a Must Play VR Exp?
reviews.review_description:
Is Human Within a Must Play VR Experience? | Full Video Review @
https://youtu.be/787PLZpLgiU

#HumanWithin by @signalspacelab is a narrative-driven experience that blends cutting-edge visuals, interactive storytelling, and intense decision-making that will keep you on the edge of your seat.  

Is this a must-play for VR enthusiasts? Watch our full review to find out!

developer_response.body: TheSil3ntX, Thank you for playing Human Within, rating your experience on Quest 2, and sharing your review on YouTube. Much appreciated.

reviews.review_title: Very entertaining with a lot to like
reviews.review_description: I'm on a Quest 2 and my favourite types of games are similar to Myst and Moss. I've played about 45 minutes of Human Within so far and I'm really enjoying it. This is my first time with 360 video and I'm quite impressed. The devs have done a good job of separating between the movie sections of the story when you are just watching and the more interactive elements where the user interface is different by design. The interface of the two different "worlds" feel natural and make sense visually. I was blown away by the concept with the shoplifter and the mechanics involved (I don't want to spoil more here). I only have two areas that I feel could be improved, both of which have been mentioned in other reviews: 1) I've had 3 cube puzzle solves so far and I can definitely see that they will get boring if they are repeated too often without much variation (slightly more difficult is not variation enough), and 2) I think I've had 2 notifications of "choice" in choosing the story direction, they came out of nowhere (which is fine), but I ran out of time before knowing what to do. I would recommend that for the earliest "choices", give a lot more opportunity for the player to understand what is happening. Nevertheless, I look forward to finishing the game and trying at least one different ending. -Csaba

developer_response.body: Hello Csaba42! So happy to hear you have been enjoying your playthrough! Regarding the time allocated for the choice moments, we did strive to balance this with ensuring a well paced and seamless experience. That being said by looking away and returning your gaze back to the options the clock resets (apprx 4 secs for each choice). Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: I  enjoyed this game, it was like watching a movie and being a part of it, I will play through it again to see how my answers change the outcome!

developer_response.body: Happy to hear you enjoyed your initial playthrough Giddy.Up. Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description: I'd like to encourage more effort like this. Thanks for the work you put into this. Still a growing entertainment niche on the bleeding edge of technology. More things to get "right", looking forward to see more advancements.

developer_response.body: Thank you Rehyrts! Our goal was to help evolve storytelling in the genre and entertain while doing so. We appreciate the review and support for this project!

reviews.review_description:
I found this an intriguing interactive narrative movie game. The acting was fairly good and tight , but movie itself could be a little longer and have  more action , including fight and romance  scenes .   2d graphics were pretty sharp, but the 360 scenes were not so sharp on my oculus 2 anyways .( I guess the tech is just not there yet ? )

Still I gave it 4 stars because of the intriguing concept and gutsy effort to produce this kind of media. It's mind bending to me that  Hollywood hasn't launched en masse into this new medium yet. If not in interactive sense , at least beyond traditional 2d into full 360 immersion . That would be truly exciting.

But in meantime , kudos to the independent and smaller producers , who are pioneering this new kind of enhanced movie game experiences . 

I hope more folks support this kind of effort , so the variety and quality just keeps getting better and better.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and support, infinity.drive.2024! We appreciate the feedback on the technical aspects and hope your interest and curiosity thus far leads to additional playthroughs so you can experience the multiple outcomes to this story based on the different choices you make.
